title: Fix emoji-width alignment bugs in visual.py box rendering
source: Unicode terminal rendering — WCWIDTH standard for character display widths
description: visual.py uses ljust() for column alignment (lines 233, 243, 274, etc.), but Python's ljust() counts characters, not display width. Emoji like 🧪 and 🛡️ occupy 2 terminal columns but count as 1-2 characters, causing all boxes to misalign. This is visible in every rendered board — right borders don't line up. The fix is straightforward with wcwidth or a simple helper function.
proposed_changes: Add a `display_width(s: str) -> int` helper using unicodedata.east_asian_width() to calculate true terminal display width of strings containing emoji. Then add `pad_to_width(s: str, width: int) -> str` that pads with spaces based on display width instead of character count. Replace all ljust() calls in render_team_board(), render_sprint_dashboard(), and render_morty_box() with pad_to_width(). Alternatively, add `wcwidth` as an optional dependency for more accurate width calculation. This immediately fixes the broken box borders visible in every visual.py output.
